perturbation.original_text: The undersigned agrees that he ---------------------------------- will not sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of his interests in, or reduce his risk relative to, any of the shares of PSS Common Stock into which his shares of GSMS Common Stock are converted upon consummation of the Merger until such time as PSS notifies the undersigned that the requirements of SEC Accounting Series Release Nos. 130 and 135 ("ASR 130 and 135") have been met.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned agrees that he will endeavor not to sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of his interests in, or reduce his risk relative to, any of the shares of PSS Common Stock into which his shares of GSMS Common Stock are converted upon consummation of the Merger until such time as PSS, at its convenience, notifies the undersigned that the requirements of SEC Accounting Series Release Nos. 130 and 135 ("ASR 130 and 135") have been purportedly met.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will not sell' to 'will endeavor not to sell' weakens the restriction on disposition. Adding 'at its convenience' makes the notification by PSS discretionary. Adding 'purportedly met' introduces doubt as to whether the requirements have truly been met.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 - Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity and discretion, which could allow for insider trading or unfair advantage based on non-public information, contradicting the aim of SEC regulations to ensure fair and transparent markets. Delaying notification at PSS's convenience could allow some affiliates to trade before others, based on when they receive the notification, thus violating the tenets of Rule 10b-5.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'will not sell' to 'will endeavor not to sell,' coupled with the added discretion for PSS to notify the undersigned 'at its convenience' and the doubt introduced by 'purportedly met,' weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity. This could facilitate actions that operate as a fraud or deceit upon a person in connection with the purchase or sale of any security, potentially violating 17 CFR § 240.10b-5(c). The original text created a firm obligation while the modified version creates discretion and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: PSS agrees that it will publish such results as promptly as practicable following the Merger in the sole discretion of PSS, but in any event within 45 days after the end of the first fiscal quarter of PSS containing the required period of post-Merger combined operations and that it will notify the undersigned promptly following such publication.
perturbation.changed_text: PSS will use its best judgement to attempt to publish such results as soon as feasible following the Merger, subject to internal resource availability, and will try to notify the undersigned shortly after such a potential publication.
perturbation.explanation: The original text sets a deadline of 45 days after the end of the first fiscal quarter. The modified text removes the hard deadline, replacing it with subjective criteria such as 'best judgement,' 'as soon as feasible,' and 'internal resource availability'. This removes a clearly defined time frame for publishing the financial results which can be deemed misleading by some.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 11 imposes liability for false or misleading statements in a registration statement. By weakening the commitment to timely and transparent financial reporting, this modification creates an environment where misleading information (or delayed information) could be provided to investors, hindering their ability to make informed decisions and potentially leading to violations of Section 11 if those financial results ultimately become part of a registration statement.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 15 / CHAPTER 2A / SUBCHAPTER I / § 77k Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 15 USC 77k : Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 15-COMMERCE AND TRADE CHAPTER 2A-SECURITIES AND TRUST INDENTURES SUBCHAPTER I-DOMESTIC SECURITIES Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Amendments Effective Date §77k. Civil liabilities on account of false registration statement (a) Persons possessing cause of action; persons liable In case any part of the registration statement, when such part became effective, contained an untrue statement of a material fact or omitted to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the state
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the original commitment to timely financial reporting by replacing the fixed deadline with subjective criteria, potentially leading to delayed or misleading information. The original text states results will be published "within 45 days after the end of the first fiscal quarter", while the modified text replaces this with "as soon as feasible following the Merger, subject to internal resource availability". The Law Explanation states this "creates an environment where misleading information (or delayed information) could be provided to investors". Scraped Law Snippet 1, 15 USC 77k, discusses civil liabilities for false registration statements, indicating that untrue statements of material fact or omissions of material fact can lead to legal action. The change increasing ambiguity can be seen as increasing the likelihood of unintentionally creating an untrue statement or omitting one, in violation of 15 USC 77k. This falls under the Ambiguity Legal Contradiction definition because it introduces an ambiguous legal obligation, making enforcement difficult and potentially leading to non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: If the undersigned desires to sell or -------------------------- otherwise transfer the shares of PSS Common Stock received by him in connection with the Merger at any time during the restrictive period set forth in Rule 145(d), the undersigned will provide the necessary representation letter to the transfer agent for PSS Common Stock together with such additional information as the transfer agent may reasonably request. If PSS's counsel concludes that such proposed sale or transfer complies with the requirements of Rule 145(d), PSS shall cause such counsel to provide such opinions as may be necessary to PSS's Transfer Agent so that the undersigned may complete the proposed sale or transfer.
perturbation.changed_text: If the undersigned wishes to explore the possibility of selling or otherwise transferring the shares of PSS Common Stock received in connection with the Merger, the undersigned may consider providing a representation letter to the transfer agent. PSS's counsel may, at their discretion, review the proposed sale or transfer, and potentially offer non-binding guidance.
perturbation.explanation: The original text outlines a process for selling/transferring shares under Rule 145(d). The modified text introduces uncertainty by replacing 'desires to sell' with 'wishes to explore the possibility', 'will provide' with 'may consider providing', 'PSS shall cause such counsel to provide such opinions' with 'PSS's counsel may, at their discretion, review... and potentially offer non-binding guidance'. This makes the process discretionary rather than obligatory for PSS.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 145 provides a framework for reselling securities acquired in mergers. This modification undermines the Rule by making the process discretionary, potentially hindering an affiliate's ability to sell shares legally under Rule 145(d) due to the uncertainty introduced by the revised language, thus creating an artificial barrier to their legal right to sell.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 230.145 - Reclassification of securities, mergers, consolidations and acquisitions of assets. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.145 - Reclassification of securities, mergers, consolidations and acquisitions of assets. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.145 Reclassification of securities, mergers, consolidations and acquisitions of assets. Preliminary Note: Rule 145 ( § 230.145 of this chapter) is designed to make available the protection provided by registration under the Securities Act of 1933 , as amended (Act), to persons who are offered securities in a business combination of the type described in paragraphs (a) (1), (2) and (3) of the rule. The thrust of the rule is that an offer, offer to sell, offer for sale, or sale occurs when there is submitted to security holders a plan or agreement pursuant to which such holders are required to elect, on the basis of what is in substance a new investment decision, whether to accept a new or different security in exchange for their existing security . Rule 145 embodies the Commission 's determination that such transactions are subject to the registration requirements of the Act , and that the previously existing no-sale theory of Rule 133 is no longer consistent with the statutory purposes of the Act . See Release No. 33-5316 (October 6, 1972) [ 37 FR 23631 ]. Securities issued in transactions described in paragraph (a) of Rule 145 may be registered on Form S-4 or F-4 (§ 239.25 or § 239.34 of this chapter) or Form N-14 ( § 239.23 of this chapter) under the Act . Transactions for which statutory exemptions under the Act , including those contained in sections 3(a)(9), (10), (11) and 4(2), are otherwise available are not affected by Rule 145. Reference is made to Rule 153a ( § 230.153a of this chapter) describing the prospectus delivery required in a transaction of the type referred to in Rule 145. A r
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and makes compliance with Rule 145 discretionary, whereas the original text outlined a mandatory process. The modification replaces 'will provide' with 'may consider providing' and 'PSS shall cause such counsel to provide such opinions' with 'PSS's counsel may, at their discretion, review... and potentially offer non-binding guidance'. This undermines the framework provided by Rule 145. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, 'Rule 145 ( § 230.145 of this chapter) is designed to make available the protection provided by registration under the Securities Act of 1933...'. By making the process discretionary, the revised text reduces the protection afforded to individuals selling shares acquired in mergers.
